subject: Natural Sciences
title: Solar System
tagline: Explore the planets, moons and stars of our solar system!

pins.name: Mercury
facts: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.
Mercury is the smallest planet in the solar system.

pins.name: Venus
facts: Venus is the hottest planet in the solar system.
Venus is often called Earth’s twin because of its similar size.

pins.name: Earth
facts: Earth is the only known planet with life.
Earth takes 365 days to orbit the Sun.

pins.name: Mars
facts: Mars is known as the Red Planet.
Mars has the largest volcano in the solar system, Olympus Mons.

pins.name: Jupiter
facts: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system.
Jupiter has a giant storm called the Great Red Spot.

pins.name: Saturn
facts: Saturn is famous for its bright rings.
Saturn's rings are made mostly of ice and rock.

pins.name: Uranus
facts: Uranus spins on its side compared to other planets.
Uranus appears blue-green due to methane gas.

pins.name: Neptune
facts: Neptune is the farthest planet from the Sun.
Neptune has the strongest winds in the solar system.

questions.question: Which one is true: "Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun."?
questions.feedbackFact: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.
